title: After BCU blocks system reboot the uninstall progress window stops updating and uninstallers stop running
Empty folder after official uninstaller completion is not detected as a leftover
body:
Hey, would be nice to detect empty folders which was left after official uninstallation.

For example, I've installed Supersonic Sled NVIDIA demo, then uninstalled it.
Official uninstaller clears the demo folder from the C:\Program Files (x86)\NVIDIA Corporation\NVIDIA Demos, but this parent folder is left empty and it's not detected as a leftover.

Maybe, leftovers search could have a pre-uninstall pass to be able to detect such cases?
Like Analyse app > uninstall it > analyse leftovers with consideration of the initial analysis.

This additional pass could be probably used in some other cases to improve leftovers detection.

title: Interesting application relationship detection issue
body:
Hi, first off I love BC Uninstaller, I seriously think it's my favorite application for Windows. Great stuff here and thank you for all the work that goes into it :)

I have had this happen with a few random applications before in the past with no real pattern in their category. When chosen to uninstall it will proceed to the step where you can kill potential blocker apps, and the list will be pretty much everything running at the moment in Windows. Continuing past that with no killing everything proceeds smoothly  and app is uninstalled.

When searching for leftover crap I am presented with a list of the regular items BC-U finds like Start Menu lnks, prefetch data, and what appears to be a nice sized chunk of my Windows Registry; all check marked for removal with a confidence rating of: Good.

This very well could be completely a problem with the application(s) themselves and how they react with BC-U, etc. etc. But I thought I'd bring this to attention in case it hasn't been observed before and since it is potentially a loaded gun for a unsuspecting user.

I exported the list here for you and if there is anything else I could give you logs related I'd be happy to do so. I failed to screen grab but might be able to replicate this once more. 

My system:
Razer Blade Stealth 13" 2020 model
i7-1065G7 4C-8T CPU
16GBDDR4
WIN 11 Pro x64
[New BCUninstaller Junk Export.txt](https://github.com/Klocman/Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller/files/10308586/New.BCUninstaller.Junk.Export.txt)

title: WindowsApps (Microsoft Store)
body:
Friendly reminder: Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller does not remove older versions of apps installed from the Microsoft Store App.

Microsoft Store apps Folders;
- C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\
- C:\Users\{user}\AppData\Local\Packages\

```bash
# List Microsoft Store apps for all users
Get-AppxPackage -AllUsers | Sort-Object Name | Select-Object PackageFullName, Name, Version

# List Microsoft Store apps for a specific user (replace {user} with your username)
Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\{user}\AppData\Local\Packages\" | ForEach-Object { $PackageName = $_.Name; Get-AppxPackage | Where-Object { $_.PackageFamilyName -like "*$PackageName*" } | Sort-Object Name | Select-Object PackageFullName, Name, Version }
```

![Screenshot 2023-11-21 125657](https://github.com/Klocman/Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller/assets/12288837/6cebec1c-b84a-44d7-9e22-2208a8ea67c9)

title: BCU wants to close just about every running background program when uninstalling
body:
I've used BCU for years and when it gets to the "kill tasks" part, it always only wants you to close out of relevant programs. After updating to 5.8.2 from I have no idea what version I was on before, it now wants me to kill just about every program under the sun when uninstalling things that are completely unrelated to it. I wanted to uninstall a file compare tool that I only wanted to use once and didn't need anymore (which I've used and uninstalled with BCU before in the past), and it wants to kill literally every single program I have running, whether it be firefox, to everything (the search program), to Nvidia background stuff, to Discord, MSI afterburner, Steam, windows explorer, down to my damn UPS software, pretty much just open task manager and anything in there that isn't imperative to windows usage, it's in the "kill" list.

I'm not sure if this is intentional, but I find it really odd that it does that now when it didn't before so I can only assume it's an unintentional bug.

Hello.
The situation was like this on Windows.
Installed BCU on an account with administrator rights (ADMIN). Then I decided to create a new account (ADMIN2) with administrator rights. And give the ADMIN account the rights of a regular user.

Now when I launch BCU on the ADMIN account, I do not see any new installed programs. 
Perhaps the problem is that due to the change in rights, most programs are now installed in a different directory.

In advance, thank you for your help!

title: Almost had to deal with irreparable damage to my computer
body:
I tried to uninstall an audio plugin to solve a problem I had with registration, and while trying do a deep clean after, it ended up suggesting I delete a long list of registry keys. Some of them looked legit so I went on with it, but I didn't notice there were MANY more than I expected. It actually deleted so many stuff I couldn't access any audio controllers, many apps that are not even remotely related to the one I uninstalled became inaccessible, even 7zip didn't work properly. It also didn't make a restore point when it said it did. I was using the portable version a lot before, I used the newest version now and it was a complete train wreck, nothing like what I experienced before.

I had a restore point from a Windows update that solved the problem for me, luckily enough, but this was a serious problem that could have made me unable to do my daily job and impact my life seriously for a while. I won't share more info at this point because I don't know if I'm being extremely dumb or didn't read something vital (I think I did) or something so I'll wait to see about that before giving more details, if someone cares enough to help. If this qualifies as just a complaint I'm sorry, I still just want to share a really bad user experience I just had so at least I can understand how this came out so wrong. Thank you.

title: BCUninstaller Removed Shared Settings Folder for Multiple Blender Versions
body:
Hello,

I tried BCUninstaller for a while and it was very good, but I encountered an issue. I had both Blender versions 4.2 and 4.4 installed. I used the program to uninstall version 4.4, and it was precise in the uninstallation. However, the problem I faced is that it deleted the settings located at:

> `..\AppData\Roaming\Blender Foundation\Blender\`

It should have only removed the settings for Blender 4.4 and not treated the settings folders for each version as a single entry in the junk/leftover list.

**Suggestions to improve the accuracy of handling leftovers after uninstallation:**

1. Add an option to install programs through BCUninstaller while monitoring file and registry changes during the installation.

2. Add a registry cleaner feature to manually clean leftovers without needing to uninstall the program. This would be useful in cases where the program was uninstalled normally (without using any uninstaller tool).

title: When the installer selects a non-English language, the program remains in English after installation if the "Extra Languages" option is unchecked
body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1cce13da-062a-40b2-ad82-5e5f944c0d2e)

When the installer selects Chinese as the installation language and the "Extra Languages" option is unchecked, the program will only be in English after installation, rather than the language selected during setup. This is somewhat counterintuitive.

It is recommended that when selecting the installation language, if a non-English language is chosen, the default built-in language should be the selected language instead of English. If the English language pack is a necessary component of the program, it is suggested that even when the "Extra Languages" option is unchecked, the selected installation language can still be installed simultaneously.

title: Install date questionable
body:
Hi, 

Today, 5 Aug, I was testing BCU by installing a couple of steam games which I was then going to remove. After installation I sorted the BCU list view by descending Install date, expecting to see the latest games at, or at least near, to the top of the list. In fact I had to scroll down several pages before I got to them, and noticed that the install date column reported the games as being installed yesterday.

In addition, there seemed to be many entries dated 4 Aug which were actually installed considerably before then. Seems that Install date is not a reliable indicator of when the app was, well, installed!

title: "The feature you are trying to use is on a network resource that is unavailable." pop-up window in v6.1.0
body:
On some systems a pop-up message might appear one or more times during initial application search asking for a network resource path. Closing the window lets BCU continue to load with no ill effects, so it's merely an annoyment.

Previous discussion https://github.com/Klocman/Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller/issues/860#issuecomment-4016337363

> This is the dialogue in question:
> 
> Windows Installer
>
>The feature you are trying to use is on a network resource that is unavailable.
>
>Click OK to try again, or enter an alternate path to a folder containing the installation package 'vba6.msi' in the box below.
>
>Use source:
>X:\PLACEHOLDER
> 
> Eventually, BCU loaded. The dialogue does not appear anymore.
> Do you know how and why it took such a long time and doesn't reappear?
> 
> Many thanks! 

> @piknockyou Then it is specifically a v6.1 issue, it's caused by Msiexec enumeration as I've mentioned. It takes a long time for the same reason I bet.
> 
> I thought I avoided the "network resource unavailable" pop-up but I guess it still can happen, I guess you installed some application to a network drive?
> 
> Does the pop-up appear after closing and restarting BCU? If it doesn't appear then it's triggered by https://github.com/Klocman/Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller/blob/05f2c74a9c2615be16c1e30f4e6ab5bdc7f25a51/source/KlocTools/IO/MsiTools.cs#L122 but if it does appear it's probably triggered by  https://github.com/Klocman/Bulk-Crap-Uninstaller/blob/05f2c74a9c2615be16c1e30f4e6ab5bdc7f25a51/source/KlocTools/IO/MsiTools.cs#L224

It might be necessary to forgo using these APIs altogether and access the Msiexec database directly to avoid this issue. If anyone has any good ideas on how to solve this, please do let me know or even better make a PR.
